title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 12th February 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Add and hold; one of the better energy plays
recommendations.detail: For a caller sitting on a 15-16% loss, he said you can both add and hold; no need to be afraid. Called it one of the better stars in the energy space with a strong acquisition portfolio that will grow well.
Recently attempted 52-week highs and is modestly consolidating. Not a cheap stock at about 80 PE, but he expects a good result (tomorrow, the 13th) and advised buying after the result.

Said there is an operation/rigging by an operator; above 300 it has distributed heavily. He wouldn't be surprised if it fell to 200. The sector and order book are good, but as a stock be wary; only sustains if it trades and holds above 280 on volume.

recommendations.note: Positional buy up to the NSE IPO
recommendations.detail: Result hit it out of the park and the stock is at an all-time high. As a positional trade up to the NSE IPO, he said BSE will sustain and to buy it. A dip toward 3,000 (gap-fill) is a good buy point.
Momentum is reappearing lately and the stock's order booking is extremely good, so hold. Flagged that the group's RCB (IPL team) bid could have some impact on the group.

recommendations.note: Hold; recovered to 700, strong above it
recommendations.detail: After the promoter stake sale the stock fell to about 570-600 and recovered back to 700, which shows its strength. He said it will be very strong once it clears 700 again; hold.

recommendations.note: Hold; transforming into a defense company
recommendations.detail: Good results; post-results it is consolidating strongly above 70. It is slowly transforming from an optical-fiber company into a defense company, and he wouldn't be surprised if it attempts 100 again. Hold.

recommendations.note: Shift to Reliance; software disruption risk
recommendations.detail: Suggested shifting from TCS to Reliance, which he sees as safer. Software's Nifty weight is falling and AI disruption hits Indian IT too; TCS is near a 52-week low and would need to cross 3,200-3,300 to recover, which doesn't look immediate. Be cautious in service-oriented software stocks across the globe.

recommendations.note: Buy only with a long-term view
recommendations.detail: Near a 52-week low and a sin stock the government keeps eyeing. Even as a diversified FMCG company, he said buy only if you have a long-term view; in a short-term position don't expect big returns.
Near a 52-week low with weak December-quarter results; he advised against averaging the caller's 50% loss. Financial performance is not promising at all, so exit on rallies.

recommendations.note: Wait for today's result before buying
recommendations.detail: Has a result today, so he said it is better to buy after seeing the result rather than now.
Described it as a market proxy that tracks broad swings (e.g. Adani stocks). Now a consistent high-dividend payer, so hold, but with an extended long-term view.

recommendations.note: High debt is working capital, no concern
recommendations.detail: On a viewer's debt worry, he explained the debt is mostly working capital, normal for services firms. Borrowings of 175 crores against trade receivables of 185 crores, with neutral net cash flows; income growing and margins improving. Nothing to worry about, just invest and hold.
Strong result; the stock hit 1,070, a 52-week high. He expects a modest correction toward 930-940 and said to accumulate at that level. Existing holders can hold.

recommendations.note: Stay in Canfin, don't shift to RBL/AU
recommendations.detail: Canfin is still strong with good post-result performance, so he would not suggest switching out of it (to RBL Bank or AU Small Finance Bank). Noted that AU, converting from small-finance to a regular bank and consolidating near 1,000, is momentum-wise currently the stronger name.
Caller in 20% profit. For short-term players he advised booking some short-term profit, at least 50%; long-term players should keep a trading stop. Definitely book some profit here.
Caller in 20% profit; he gave it the same treatment as Siemens Energy, advising short-term players to book part of the profit and long-term players to keep a trading stop.

Said there are much better companies; treat it as speculative only or for those tracking inside info. Name changed to Valor Estate; came below 100 and mildly recovered on December results. Prefer frontline realty names like Brigade or Prestige.

Held at 180; he suggested it yesterday too. As of now hold, but reduce the position when the market comes back above 25,900, because indications of crossing 26,000 are not appearing this series, which feels tight.
Said there is no loss at all and to accumulate; the stock has strong consolidation between 300 and 335. Definitely accumulate.
